title: Magnetically controllable device
abstract: A magnetically controllable device is described comprising a body (10) defining a fluid flow channel (12), a first magnetic material particle (16) located within the flow channel (12) and rigidly secured to a wall (14) defining the flow channel (12), and a second magnetic material particle (20) located within the flow channel (12) and moveable relative to the first particle (16), the first and second particles (16, 20) having different magnetic anisotropies at least in the direction of an axis upon which both of the particles lie. Also described is a magnetically controllable device comprising a flexible substrate (30) upon which a plurality of first particles (16), (16') are located and upon which a plurality of second particles (20, 20') are located, the first particles (16, 16') being of a different magnetic anisotropy to the second particles (20, 20') such that the application of a varying magnetic field to the device causes relative movement between the first particles (16, 16') and the second particles (20, 20'), the substrate (30) flexing to accommodate such relative movement.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS: 1. A magnetically controllable device comprising a body defining a fluid flow channel, a first magnetic material particle located within the flow channel and rigidly secured to a wall defining the flow channel, and a second magnetic material particle located within the flow channel and moveable relative to the first particle, the first and second particles having different magnetic anisotropies at least in the direction of an axis upon which both of the particles lie. 2. A device according to Claim 1 , wherein the second particle is directly coupled to the first particle by an elastic coupling member. 3. A device according to Claim 1 wherein the second particle is coupled to the wall of the flow channel by an elastic coupling member. 4. A device according to any of the preceding claims, further comprising one or more additional magnetic material particles. 5. A device according to Claim 4, wherein one or more additional first particles are rigidly secured to the flow channel wall. 6. A device according to Claim 4 or Claim 5, wherein one or more additional second particles are located within the flow channel. 7. A device according to Claim 6, wherein the additional second particles are movable relative to respective additional first particles. 8. A device according to any of the preceding claims, wherein the locations of the particles and the additional particles in the flow channel is chosen so that the device may serve as a valve. 9. A device according to Claim 8, wherein the flow channel is of is of forked form, particles being associated with each limb of the flow channel such that control over the device allows selective supply of fluid along selected limbs of the flow channel. 10. A device according to any of the preceding claims, wherein the locations of the particles and the additional particles in the flow channel is chosen so that the device may serve as a mixer or agitator. 1 1. A device according to Claim 10, wherein the flow channel is shaped to define a mixing chamber in which a plurality of the particles are located. 12. A magnetically controllable device comprising a flexible substrate upon which a plurality of first particles are located and upon which a plurality of second particles are located, the first particles being of a different magnetic anisotropy to the second particles such that the application of a varying magnetic field to the device causes relative movement between the first particles and the second particles, the substrate flexing to accommodate such relative movement. 13. A device according to Claim 12, wherein the substrate takes the form of a mesh. 14. A device according to Claim 12 or Claim 13, wherein the substrate is of nonuniform stiffness. 15. A device according to any of Claims 12 to 14, wherein the substrate is shaped to be of tubular form, spherical form, or to adopt another three dimensional shape. 16. A device according to any of Claims 12 to 15, wherein the substrate defines a plurality of pores, and relative movement of the particles, in use, causes flexing of the substrate such that a fluid is delivered through the pores. 17. A device according to Claim 16, wherein the pores are sufficiently small that when not subject to a varying magnetic field, the surface tension of the fluid resists the delivery of fluid through the pores. 18. A magnetically controllable device comprising a first particle and a plurality of second particles connected to the first particle, the first particle being of a different magnetic anisotropy to the second particles such that the application of a varying 

 magnetic field to the device causes relative movement between the second particles and the first particle. 19. A magnetically controllable device comprising a body defining a fluid flow channel, a first magnetic material particle located within the flow channel and secured to a wall defining the flow channel by a flexible element. 20. A device according to Claim 19, wherein a plurality of such particles are located within the flow channel.
